desc:
The river flows swiftly south, gaining speed as it reaches a hilly section of
the forest. The banks are too steep to allow climbing up the shore and there is
no choice but to swim along. As the waters rush onward, the river appears to
bend east, gently turning at the outskirts of a golden forest.
name: Celebrant

desc:
From the edge of this peaceful forest one can look and marvel at the harmonic
view of the River Celebrant. A steep bank runs down toward the river. The clear
glittering waters coming from the northwest make a turn and rush due east,
passing a natural arch formed by two trees on either shore.
name: At the Bank of the River Celebrant

desc:
On a small hill to the south stands a majestic tree arching over the swift
river, where it meets the crown of another tree growing on the opposite shore of
the Celebrant. The two trees form a natural bridge over the water; however,
crossing the strong branches doesn't seem that easy, as opposed to descending
into the peaceful forest to the east.
name: At the Majestic Arching Tree

desc:
The forest around is quite sparse. To the southwest a majestic tree has bent
across the river and entwined with another such natural pillar on the other
shore. It might not be the best bridge over the rushing waters to the south, yet
there appears to be no evil that would dare plant here trickery, throwing you
into the swift Celebrant. It just might be that in the end it is the skill of
the climber that will count.
name: East of the Majestic Tree

desc:
The trees and grass of this area seem to have been woven from ancient lore, no
longer being born in this world. The trunks of the mellyrn are coloured a
gleaming silvery grey and their roots are covered with last spring's golden
leaves. Together with the sparkling river to the south, the forest forms a
picture of nearly perfect harmony. The waters of the river might seem tempting,
but it would be folly to challenge destiny and plunge down from the high rising
bank.
name: Amidst the Golden Forest

desc:
The mallorn-trees here are of varying shades of gold and silver, their bark
rough to the eye yet smooth to the touch, their colour somewhat reserved yet
glorious, their wisdom unseen, yet striking anyone with awe. Water flickers
through the tangled branches to the south, while in all the other directions,
the golden forest continues.
name: Amidst the Golden Forest

desc:
Trees shift their branches and rustle their leaves, whispering stories as old as
the great seas rumoured to lie many journeys west of here. What words of wisdom
they exchange, what mysteries they unveil, one cannot tell. To the south the
Celebrant flows unhindered eastwards. The river bank is very steep, and the tree
roots climbing out of the bank do not seem to be strong enough to support the
weight of anyone climbing down into the water.
name: Whispering Forest

desc:
This opening in the forest might appear to be a good place to climb down into
the refreshing waters to the south. The shore, however, has not fashioned itself
according to the desires of a weary traveller. The tree-roots could be used to
reach the Celebrant, yet one cannot be sure of safe return to this bank of the
river. In all other directions the golden forest continues.
name: At the Root-tangle

desc:
The golden forest is quite dense here, at the northern shore of the River
Celebrant. Branches seem to make way for a good-spirited traveller east and west
but block the way to the south. Perhaps it is nature itself protecting the
unwary by making it impossible to step down from the steep bank of the
Celebrant? The river can be heard flowing in the south, in all other directions
the mallorn forest continues.
name: Amidst the Golden Forest

desc:
The trees that line this section of the river bend inwards, forming a series of
bridges across the running water. In some spots the trees completely block the
daytime sunlight, laying deep shadows across the river. In other places the
trees are less frequent and the sky can be seen above the Celebrant that flows
slowly eastward.
name: Bend in the River

desc:
Flowing lazily between two lines of trees the river Celebrant slowly changes its
direction from east to north. Some leaves and broken tree-branches can be seen
floating on the surface of the water. Other branches that failed to navigate the
sharp curve have been washed up onto the eastern riverbank and collected there,
forming a rather large pile.
name: Bend in the River

desc:
The gentle slope of the land is broken by a sharp dip which becomes nearly
vertical just before the land joins the river to the west and north. While
little effort is required to fall into the river, obtaining a foothold on dry
land from the water will be difficult. Some of the trees grow on the edge of the
bank where the land drops into the river, while others, which once grew where
the river now flows, have fallen and begun to rot.
name: The Forest near the River

desc:
The forest, containing mostly beeches and mellyrn, is quite dense. Several
varieties of low-lying vegetation have grown into what little open space once
lay between the ancient trees. Despite the dense growth of bushes, a river can
be seen to the west, flowing lazily between the trees. A huge rock lies on the
ground among the tree roots. The rock is weathered and covered with grey lichen.
name: The Forest near the River

desc:
A few elm trees can be seen growing here. Their number rapidly increases to the
north where the forest obscures your vision. Even though you cannot see beyond
the trees, the faint sounds of a distant river can be heard from that direction.
A faint trail leads to the east, towards the borders of the shimmering Golden
Wood. If you were to study the trail closely, it would seem to be fading even as
you look at it. A wide but shallow mound of soft dirt forms the nest for a
colony of ants against the side of one of the smaller trees.
name: The Forest near the River

desc:
The silvery trees rise high into the skies, murmuring softly as the wind moves
through their branches. The surrounding ground is uneven, sloping up and down.
One of the mallorn-trees is much larger than the others and has a much thicker
foliage. The foliage of this tree is more than capable of concealing the
dwellings of Elves, who are known for building their homes in the trees in order
to escape the dangers of the forest.
name: The Golden Forest

desc:
The dense forest continues in all directions, filled with a variety of bushes
and trees. On this spot, however, many of the plants appear to have been smashed
by an inhuman strength. It is possible the damage was done by a storm, though
the pattern of destruction is unlike that of a common storm. Regardless of the
cause of the destruction, many trees lie dead upon the grass like corpses after
a battle. The ground, sloping up and down, remains covered by a soft green grass
which escaped the destruction that did not spare the larger plants.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
This is the middle of an ancient forest full of very tall and thick trees.
Judging by their height and girth, many of the trees appear older than the
oldest living creatures on Arda. Their gentle whispering fills the forest and
sounds much like a song. The soft grassy ground is filled with flowers, many
with blooms that appear brighter than the stars during the dark nights.
name: The Golden Forest

desc:
These fields are dry but still manage to support a wide variety of grasses and
plants. Many tiny insects can be seen feeding on the lush grasses. West of here
the ground begins to slope upwards where it eventually forms a small mound.
Except for that mound, no landmarks are visible in this otherwise featureless
terrain.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The ground here is slightly higher than the surroundings. A soft layer of grass
dotted by many weeds and flowers covers this elevated area. To the north and
east nothing of significance can be seen. To the west the silhouette of a small
wooden shack is visible.
name: Small Mound

desc:
It appears that much of this land has been cleared, as only a few fresh blades
of grass can be seen sprouting from the fertile ground. A small wooden shack is
standing here; it appears to be some sort of sentry post. To the east a small
mound rises from the plains, and to the northwest, where the grass is quite
thin, a huge boulder is visible. In all other directions the vast grass-covered
plains continue with no interruptions or landmarks.
name: Sentry Post

desc:
The ground is dry, dusty and is composed almost exclusively of compacted rock.
While a few clumps of soil can be seen between the rocky plates, they are too
small and too shallow to support plant growth. To the north a very large boulder
can be seen. Except for the boulder and a small wooden structure towards the
east, no landmarks break the monotony of these desolate plains.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
The ground here is solid rock, covered with a thin layer of dirt and sand. The
shallow soil makes it impossible for any plants to take root and survive. The
lack of plant roots allows the slightest wind to create a wall of sand that
obscures all vision. To the west are the remains of a number of structures which
appear to have once formed a thriving village. In all other directions nothing
but empty plains can be seen.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
This area appears to be the ruins of a once busy village. While a few buildings
are still standing, most have been reduced to nothing more than piles of rubble
and dust. While it is impossible to determine exactly when the village fell into
ruins, it clearly happened many years ago for moss has overgrown most of the
rubble.
name: Ruined Village

desc:
This region is dry and barren. For years plants grew in harmony upon the land,
but now they grow here no longer. First the rain stopped, then the soil turned
hard and one by one the plants withered and died. When the rains returned, the
soil was unable to hold the water. While a few plants took root while the soil
was moist, most were unable to live until the next rain. To the west a group of
trees rises among the plains, their lush foliage seeming out of character
compared to the rest of the visible region. A few ruined buildings and some
rocks piled high can be seen towards the east.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
A few maples, no more than twenty or thirty, grow here in a clump, making this
one of the few productive spots on these otherwise desolated plains. The trees
grow very close together and seem rather short for mature trees. On some of the
trees in a few spots the bark has worn off, and on other trees the living trunk
has splintered.
name: Grove of Trees

desc:
Almost no plants have managed to grow in this sterile soil - even the common
weeds and hardy grasses of the plains soon perish after they sprout. Without
plant life, there is nothing to obscure your immediate vision of these dull,
infertile plains as they extend in all directions until they meet the horizon.
Only a small clump of trees has managed to grow due east of here and provides a
break in the monotony.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
The small rise in the landscape offers a good view of the surrounding fields.
The slope ends to the east, where plains continue as far as the eye can see.
Anyone desiring the cool shade of green trees and the fragrance of lush foliage
could head north, where far off in the distance a large forest can be seen. One
can also continue westward, where the barren slope continues and passes a small
but remarkable mound.
name: Barren Slope

desc:
At this elevation, the fields are exposed to strong winds that make staying here
somewhat uncomfortable. The gusts have blown away most of the thin layer of soil
that used to cover the dry sand. Sand dominates these fields now and more
demanding plants cannot grow here anymore. The slope rises high above the
northern fields where one can spot a small patch of green.
name: Barren Slope

desc:
The ground here is a bit elevated, offering you a good view of the surrounding
fields. The darker bar of green on the northern horizon must surely be a sign of
some forest. The slope, that does not surprise with abundance of lush nature,
continues to the east and to the west. This might be a good place for setting up
a camp, for certainly no flood will ever cover this rising.
name: Barren Slope

desc:
From this vantage, one can get a good view of the fields lying north of here,
although there does not seem to be much to discover in these barren plains. Far
north lies a belt of green that might be some kind of a forest. Looking around,
it is easy to see why the few plants that grow on this slope are trampled by
horsemen. The barren land is extremely easy to travel through, and an
approaching enemy, not so uncommon in these troubled times, can be seen from a
great distance.
name: Barren Slope

desc:
The slope has reached a small but noticeable mound that rises a bit above the
northern fields. A seemingly natural wall of trees blocks the way to the top of
the mound. Waters of many springs have carved wide furrows into the earthen side
and revealed the hard stone core underneath. The slope, that continues east and
west from here, seems to have been trodden by many hoofs, suggesting that this
may have been the favourite riding path for some groups of travellers.
name: Behind the Mound

desc:
Small rocks cover the ground where earth has slid away from the side of the
mound that rises to about the same level as this slope. Occasional blades of
grass have penetrated the rubble, but on the whole these fields have little to
offer for wildlife to graze upon. This slope continues east and west, where you
can probably get a better view of the surrounding lands.
name: Behind the Mound

desc:
A beautiful view opens from this slope, which is not so impressive in height as
the mound that stands at some distance to the northeast. Far north you see a
forest covering the fields that in other directions seem barren and desolated
except for a small coppice of trees to the northwest. Closer to the coppice the
ground bears signs of intelligent activity, perhaps failed efforts to make this
soil yield crops.
name: Along the Green Slope

desc:
A few lonely birches and pines waver in the wind that carries dust and sand from
the lower desert-like fields. There seems to be an island of green to the north
and a dark green belt of trees farther in that direction. No animals or birds
can be seen here. The lack of wildlife might be the source of the strange and
eerie sensation that even this last outpost of verdure is bound to vanish.
name: Along the Green Slope

desc:
The grass-covered slope continues east and west as far as you can see. To the
north the greenery extends and forms a small grassy peninsula among the northern
barrenness. Even farther to the north you spot a green wall, the edge of the
forest of Lothlorien.
name: Along the Green Slope

desc:
Rising out of the barren plains in the west, the grass-covered slope continues
east as far as the eye can see. Occasional winds blow up clouds of dust from the
northern sandy fields, where no trees can be seen and probably only few animals
manage to subsist. Far north there appears to be the start of a thick forest.
Looking east, you can see the bar of green extend a bit northward.
name: Along the Green Slope

desc:
The earth is bare here as there is almost no grass. Most of the topsoil has been
carried away by the wind, since there is nothing here to keep it rooted. Few
signs of animal life can be seen and no vegetation except for a few tough weeds
that continue their never-ending fight for survival. To the west and the north
the plains continue unchanged, but to the east a gentle slope climbs upward, in
its ascent slowly changing colour from ashen-grey to the soft green of grass.
name: Dry Plains

desc:
These plains are made up of mostly dry dunes of bare soil. The vegetation clings
lightly to the eroded ground. The occasional gust of wind creates clouds of sand
that fill the air, likely to deter even the most weathered traveller for a short
time. To the north, the ground appears to become less sandy.
name: Sandy Fields

desc:
Grassy hillocks, sparsely scattered across the landscape, add little variation
to these sandy fields, where the few surviving plants are stunted and seem to be
one step from losing the struggle against the intrusion of barrenness. Gazing
eastward, one cannot help but wonder if there is a mirage up ahead, mockery for
the weary traveller, or in fact a grove of trees, with real grass and, perhaps,
even water.
name: Sandy Fields

desc:
To the north you can see a lop-sided old house, its balks damaged by bark
beetles and its door about to fall off the hinges. Pelts have been hung on the
racks in front of the cot, suggesting that the owner uses his hunting skills to
acquire daily nourishment. That being the case, it is no wonder that poverty
echoes from the building. The island of green is too small, and the surrounding
fields too barren to provide ample game.
name: In Front of the Rustic House

desc:
Infrequent stunted birches and tall pines, indifferent to dry and sandy soil,
decorate this small island of green in the midst of infertile fields. You spot a
small cultivated corner where someone has tried, with little success, to grow
some crops. Larger stones have been moved from the cultivated land and arranged
so that they could now serve as a rest site for occasional travellers.
name: An Island of Green

desc:
The barren plains stretch out in all directions, producing few plants and
showing few features of interest. To get a better look at the surrounding fields
one could head east, where a small mound rises to some height above the plains,
or south, where a grassy slope would probably offer a spectacular view. Unlike
this barren and sandy ground, the coppice to the west offers cool shade of
stunted trees.
name: Plains along the Side of the Mound

desc:
Gently climbing up the eastward slope, the faint path leads to some greenery and
a large boulder. Occasional gusts of wind blow sand and dust up from the ground,
making dry air creep into your throat as you breathe. Looking west, you spot
another island of green amidst the dry and barren fields.
name: Footpath Leading up the Mound

desc:
On the top of the mound a strange boulder inlaid with writing stands encircled
by tall and graceful trees. Combining artistry with extensive, perhaps magical
knowledge of nature, careful hands have planted the trees close enough for them
to entwine and form a sturdy wall of this shrine. This small grove appears to
bear great significance to whoever has cleaned the ground of fallen branches and
wiped the dust off the rough surface of the boulder.
name: At a Rough Boulder

desc:
The surrounding terrain, still maintaining the overall impression of desolation,
varies here more in height and features. The ground rises to the south and forms
a slope, and climbs even higher to the west where a mound catches the eye. To
reach it one would have to travel around a wall of closely growing trees that
obstruct the way. Here and there junipers form small groups that get thicker and
eventually become an extensive coppice to the east. The sandy ground bears
tracks of reptiles.
name: East of the Mound

desc:
The dry grass sighs like autumn leaves rustling in the wind. The sandy soil
makes it difficult for flora to thrive, yet some tougher plants stubbornly fight
their way towards the sky. Perhaps they retrieve sustenance from damper northern
grounds; but surely not from the south, where the land seems to be even drier.
name: Withered Grass

desc:
A small patch of juniper bushes stands here, even though their stout and hardy
souls are not a common sight in these lands. Harsh conditions and an extremely
thin layer of soil do not disturb their frugal life. Their branches rustle as
wind carries sand up and at the same time blows more and more fertile ground
away. The desolate landscape rises to the south to meet a low slope.
name: At the Stunted Junipers

desc:
The ground steadily climbs upwards and forms a small but noticeable slope to the
south. The few plants that used to grow here have dried up from thirst and the
sandy winds that blow across these plains. Some bleached bones indicate that a
few animals still wander here, although there is practically no grass and
certainly no trees to find shade under.
name: Desolate Plains

desc:
The ground is hard and rocky, preventing plant life from growing on these
plains. Unobscured a lonesome large rock, covered with a brittle yellow-green
moss, can be seen clearly as it sits upon hard rocky soil. The empty, desolate
lands continue in all directions for as far as can be seen.
name: Mossy Rock

desc:
Several piles of tiny white pebbles are scattered across these otherwise
featureless fields. To the north and slightly to the west you can see an old hut
that appears decrepit and abandoned. A small but thick grove of trees lies to
the south, somewhat enlivening the otherwise desolate landscape.
name: Lifeless Plains

desc:
Most plants find it difficult to grow in the hard dry ground which is more clay
than soil. Only a few small clumps of brownish grass still struggle in their
quest for life. To the northwest a wooden cabin rises among the plains, while a
number of structures, possibly ruined buildings, can be seen to the southeast.
Below you a small section of the earth has given way forming a shallow hole.
name: Lifeless Plains

desc:
This desolate and featureless terrain continues to the east and west as far as
the eye can see. To the south a few old and half-collapsed buildings can be
found amongst piles of rubble. To the north a seemingly endless line of trees
crosses the plains.
name: Lifeless Plains

desc:
Few plants have managed to grow in this dry and arid place. Those plants, mostly
grasses, that have managed to grow on this soil are pale-brown or green and
brittle to the touch. While a line of trees can be seen to the north, much of
your vision is obscured by clouds of dust. To the south the ground becomes more
rocky and solid, while to the east, a great distance from here, a large boulder
blends in with the swirling dust.
name: Lifeless Plains

desc:
A small brook flows in from the north and slowly trickles its way towards a huge
solitary boulder, under which it vanishes. While a few plants still manage to
grow in the rocky soil, the vegetation thins rapidly towards the south where the
ground slowly turns from soil to rock. Many rocks and small stones can be seen
to the south.
name: A Large Boulder

desc:
A dense carpet of tall grasses covers these lush fields. The grasses are a
mixture of many varieties and grow rapidly with some blades over four feet high.
A huge boulder can be seen to the west where the grasslands are more sparse. To
the east and north tall grasses continue making travel in those directions
difficult, while to the south there is a small wooden hut.
name: High Grasses

desc:
The grass here is over two feet tall and wavers gently in the soft breeze.
Clumps of wild flowers dot the landscape and fill the air with their fragrance.
South of here a small mound, little more than a bump upon the plains, can be
found, while towards the southwest the shape of a small hut is visible. In all
other directions the terrain, mostly grasslands, remains unchanged.
name: High Grasses

desc:
Except for the small mound to the southwest, these grassy fields are flat for as
far as one can see. While the ground is firm and dry, the plants and grasses
find enough moisture to remain alive and healthy looking. To the north the
ground becomes more moist, perhaps from an underground stream, while to the east
conditions for plant life become less favourable. To the west the grasslands
continue seemingly unchanged.
name: Grasslands

desc:
The ground is soft and fertile supporting many trees and a variety of grasses.
The tree-branches spread out in all directions, blocking much of the view of the
sky above. To both the north and west the forest gets thicker. To the far south
a small hill rises above the plains.
name: A Light Forest

desc:
A few trees can be seen growing in the soft soil, marking the edge of a dense
forest which extends to the north and west. Most of the trees growing here are
quite young; they are the offspring of the older trees found in the forest.
Slowly but constantly the forest sows its seeds, in an attempt to extend its
borders in all possible directions.
name: A Light Forest

desc:
The forest here is very dense. Trees grow side by side with little free space
between them. The canopy formed by the branches of the trees is so dense that
the space below is devoid of all sunlight. Due to the lack of sunlight, almost
no undergrowth has managed to grow in this area. A few bushes can be seen, most
of them short and sickly-looking.
name: A Dense Forest

desc:
This dense forest appears to be as ancient as recorded history. Many of the
trees are older than the oldest dwarf. Their trunks, though twisted and scarred,
are as solid now as they were a hundred years ago. Some of the trees are thicker
than a man at his widest girth and a few are over eighty feet in height. A
barely noticeable track, most likely formed by the passage of deer and other
animals, runs east-west through the forest. The little space that did exist
between the trees has been filled by a few small bushes that struggle in the
shadows.
name: A Dense Forest

desc:
The ground here is a little lower than the surrounding area and forms the edge
of a perfectly shaped circular valley full of trees and bushes. The centre of
the bowl contains a small natural clearing. In that clearing there is an old
fire pit, which appears to have been unused for many years. The fire pit is
surrounded by a few old trunks which were most likely set there by a previous
user of the camp.
name: An Abandoned Forest Camp

desc:
The ground here slants slightly inwards from all directions. A pool of water has
collected in this depression and around it the ground is wet and spongy. This
has caused the land to be less hospitable for the trees of the golden forest.
Still this area proves difficult for travellers to traverse, for where the trees
were unable to grow, bushes quickly took root and reduced vision to a few feet.
name: The Golden Forest

desc:
Damp from the waters of the clear blue river to the north this land is densely
packed with trees. Many of the trees are ancient and quite large, though not
overly tall. The trees have many branches, some very low and some high. Those
trees that grow to the north, next to the river, have their lowest branches upon
the surface of the river and often below the surface when the river floods.
name: Woody Shore of the Celebrant

desc:
After flowing straight east several miles into the forest of Lothlorien, the
river bends northward. At the bend the water flows gently, its surface smooth
and reflective like a mirror. The reflection of the forest upon the water is so
sharp and bright that it is nearly impossible to distinguish it from the real
forest which surrounds the river.
name: Bend in the River

desc:
The water here is deep blue and very clear, allowing you to see the rocky bottom
with little difficulty. A few small fish playfully swim around the rocks and
gravel as they search for food. The river is surrounded on both sides by tall
slender trees, some of which bend across it forming a series of natural
archways. A few leaves float gently upon the water as they drift eastwards.
name: The River Celebrant

desc:
The steep bank to the north does not seem hostile, yet there seems to be no way
to reach the roots and undergrowth that would help to climb out of the water.
The golden forest to the south, rich in wildlife, offers a pleasant rest spot
for a traveller. The river flows east from here.
name: Celebrant

desc:
Here the waters lose some of the speed that they gained from the western rapids
and quietly flow east. The fry dash back and forth at the reeds decorating the
riverbank to the south. The muddy northern bank is too slippery to climb; even
the tree roots, which have tangled into a natural ladder, are too far for anyone
to reach.
name: Below the Root-tangle

desc:
In ages past, a small waterfall enthroned this area, to the pride and joy of
many elves. But as all things must come to an end, the falling waters eroded the
solid riverbed inch by inch, leading to the collapse of the top of the
waterfall. Now rapids, high rising riverbanks, and descending waters are the
only reminders of days gone past.
name: The Descending Waters

desc:
The water flows east, as it must have done from days beyond reckoning. The
somewhat mountainous riverbanks to north and south have witnessed the struggle
this river has gone through, carving its way through earth and rock. Once a
stream, now it is the beautiful Celebrant.
name: Celebrant

desc:
This part of the river is teeming with small fish. Apparently, depending on the
season, these creatures either simply pass their daily lives here or swim
upstream to lay spawn in order to continue their breed, somehow managing to find
strength to work their way against the strong current. The riverbank to the
north is very steep and offers no safe landing. The waters flow eastwards, but
to the south can be seen a natural pier extending into the water, an ideal spot
for fishing.
name: Celebrant

desc:
The river flows straight east here, emerging in the west from under a natural
arch formed by two trees on either shore. The northern bank is not too steep,
but it is impossible to reach the roots of the trees that would help you to
climb out of the water. The southern bank is low and level, the forest turf
beginning at the very edge of the water.
name: Celebrant

desc:
The river flows from the west to the east. Massive tree-trunks on either shore
prevent you from climbing out of the water. The tree-crowns meet at the top,
forming a graceful arch over the river. As far as the eye can see until the
river bends and is hidden out of sight, this appears to be the only bridge to
the secretive northern shore of the Celebrant.
name: Under the Arching Trees

desc:
The river flows from the west to the east. The bank to the north is quite steep
and there appears to be no way to reach the roots and the undergrowth that would
help a swimmer climb out of the water. The golden forest to the south, on the
other hand, seems to offer a pleasant resting spot for the weary.
name: Celebrant

desc:
The river makes a sharp turn and flows east as far as the eye can see rushing
straight and swift like the flight of an eagle. The trees to the west and south
grow gloriously golden and seem to offer a weary traveller a place to rest and
listen to their age-old time-chant.
name: Bend in the Celebrant

desc:
The sound of pounding and rushing water can be heard clearly here as the river
rushes by to the north. The forested shore has eroded slightly after taking such
a beating for many years. Plant life thrives here, fed by the fresh water. The
trees here are mostly mellyrn, and their fallen golden leaves can be seen on the
ground.
name: Forest by the River

desc:
Mellyrn grow sparsely here as the Golden Wood to the north gives way to an
ordinary forest, but their golden crowns are still visible amongst the other
trees. Small bushes grow around that surely produce berries and other fruit.
Broken branches and fallen leaves blanket the earth.
name: Forest Border

desc:
The mellyrn have become sparse, as the golden forest gives way to a beautiful
river that rushes north of here. Although the peaceful waters, the chirping of
birds and the softness of the turf would seem to make anyone want to linger
here, there is a strange yet enchanting sensation of tranquility emanating from
the other shore.
name: At the Riverside

desc:
Here the southern shore of the River Celebrant displays signs of recent
activity. The grass is unevenly cut short and the riverbank is rather trodden.
This appears to be a spot where animals of all varieties come and drink from the
waters' edge. On the other, steeper, bank grow golden trees of the same kind as
the trees on this bank. To the east you can see a graceful tree arching above
the river.
name: Trampled Riverside

desc:
A large tree arches gracefully over the swift river to the north, providing a
natural bridge to the other side of the river. Another tree can be seen on the
opposite shore, completing the path. Climbing over the branched bridge would
require steady footing.
name: At the Gracefully Arching Tree

desc:
The size of the trees decreases to the south and so does their number as they
are replaced by many small bushes and flowers. Green grass is everywhere and in
some places it has been trampled, as if many animals have wandered around here.
Even farther to the south, the trees disappear as the forest gives way to
grasslands.
name: Forest Border

desc:
Birches and mellyrn rise like the masts of sunken ships from a green and uneven
sea of ferns, hinting at the presence of water nearby. This impression is
affirmed by slurping muddy noises that emerge from under the tall plants when
anyone moves past. Here and there can be seen a number of fallen trees and rocks
covered with thick moss, untouched by nature's trimming hand.
name: Among the Ferns

desc:
The forest to the north seems to grow slowly in this direction, crawling across
the land. No bare earth can be seen as a variety of grasses completely blankets
the fertile ground. Thick knots of bushes and shrubs mix together, growing by
the base of the trees. To the south, the trees disappear completely, leading to
barren plains.
name: Wooded Plains

desc:
Sturdier conifers that have no objection to drier and sandier soil mix here with
birches, tenants of marshland forests. The ground gets considerably drier to the
south where mostly pines grow. To the north can be spotted an occasional fern,
suggesting that the descending forest is turning to marsh in that direction.
Among the pines and the birches are a few strange trees, native to no other
forest on Arda. Their crowns and boles glisten with varying shades of gold and
silver.
name: Mixed Forest

desc:
Birches and tall pines surround the immediate area. Nearby, fallen needles of
conifers have been carefully piled to construct a small anthill. Ants can be
seen scurrying along, fulfilling numerous tasks. A spine of some unwary reptile
can be seen bleaching in the anthill, trophy left by a reckless intruder. The
ground turns to marshland farther north, while becoming drier to the south.
name: At an Anthill

desc:
Tall pines and birches surround this small clearing where grass has been
trampled by countless hoofed feet of the tenants of this forest. Many animals
seem to choose this clearing as their favourite grazing spot. A few bushes grow
at the edge of the clearing, yielding berries that might fill the stomach of
other, more sentient, beings.
name: Small Clearing in the Forest

desc:
There is very little cover protecting these lands from the harsh sunlight; the
landscape here is barren and deserted. The grass is brown and wilted. Whatever
bushes and trees once grew here appear to have dried up and died, and the same
probably happened to all the flowers.
name: Dry Plains

desc:
The forest ends in a steep sandy slope. Except for the pines, little other
vegetation can be seen. Pine needles and cones carpet the ground. The wildlife
here is scarce compared to that of the northern forest, where the ground is more
fertile. The few animals that occasionally come here are witnesses to the
never-ending struggle between vibrant nature and scorched barrenness.
name: In the Sandy Forest

desc:
This appears to be the southern end of the sandy forest. Pines and a few stunted
bushes are all that grow in the sandy soil and intrusive barrenness. To the
north the forest becomes thicker and more lively. The ground climbs slowly
upwards to the south, forming a rather steep sandy slope. Except for the most
agile climbers, anyone trying to scale the slope would probably slide back into
the forest.
name: In the Sandy Forest

desc:
This seems to be one of the shadiest corners of the forest. It is impossible to
penetrate the tangled bushes to the east. To the south the ground rises and
forms a steep sandy slope, along which only the best of climbers might be able
to ascend to the fields above. Most plants have abandoned the fight to subsist
in this part of the forest. Sturdy pines are the sole survivors in this struggle
for survival, which would have ended long ago, if not for the mystical breath of
strength emanating from the forest to the north.
name: In the Sandy Forest

desc:
Mighty pines and birches block your way north and west, where sandy and
unyielding fields have become fertile soil on which these trees tower. It could
be that this forest has secrets to hide and secrets to share, but in any case
one would have to find another way to enter it. One could also travel to the
southern plains, although these seem quite barren and hostile to all forms of
life. An eye-catching mound lies to the southeast.
name: At the Edge of the Forest

desc:
The wind is very strong here, blowing dirt and dust all over. The grass and
weeds do little to protect the topsoil and fail to prevent it from being eaten
at by the strong gusts. The result is a very barren landscape, as only the
strongest grasses can survive here.
name: Dry Plains

desc:
The ground begins to slope downward to the north where, all of a sudden, it
disappears. Where one would expect to see more dunes, mighty trees spiral
towards the sky. This surprising and drastic change in scenery suggests that
some unearthly powers have put an end to the sandstorms that long claimed the
fertile lands under a reign of barrenness.
name: Sandy Fields at the Forest's Edge

desc:
Endless waves of sand extend all around you, except to the north, where the land
drops and an unexpected green forest starts below the level of the sandy fields.
As the slope is rather steep, a step in that direction might result in a swift
trip to the forest floor. These lifeless plains are in sharp contrast with the
tall trees, forming a picture that might appear unnatural to mortal eyes.
name: Sandy Fields at the Forest's Edge

desc:
Visible in the shapes of the trees and lines of the terrain, there appears to be
a link between a coppice far to the south and the outskirts of the northern
forest. So drastic is the change in scenery from the barren landscape to the
lush forest, it suggests some unearthly power put a stop to the moving sand
preventing it from encroaching upon the tree-covered land.
name: Sandy Fields at the Forest's Edge

desc:
These sandy fields lie between many different forms of terrain. The few patches
of grass here are dried and withered, in stark contrast to the mighty forest
that lies northward, and in these level plains one would not quite expect to
find a high mound emerging from the ground to the southeast. To the southwest
lies a coppice of trees that is probably better at supporting life than the
southwestern barrenness.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
Scenery on both sides of the path is somewhat bleak. Infrequent tussocks and
plants extend from the dry sandy soil, providing little solace for weary
travellers. The faint path is lined with small white rocks gathered from the
nearby fields, but except for this, no other sign of life or activity can be
seen. The path continues north and east. At some distance north one can see a
green forest, and to the south the ground rises and forms a small mound.
name: Footpath between the Mound and the Forest

desc:
The sparse vegetation that grows here looks almost dead. Trees that normally
would stand straight and tall have become dwarfed, stooping over so that a tall
human might reach greater heights than their tops. Winds whip up the topsoil and
dust from the earth as it erodes.
name: Dry Plains

desc:
The dunes continue north and south. Any traveller through this barren terrain
will soon face the onslaught of fatigue, as tiredness fetters the feet and dry
air makes the throat yearn for cold and refreshing water. Faraway trees lie to
the north, and the landscape slopes upwards to the south - both directions
offering livelier scenery than this desolate place.
name: Sandy Fields

desc:
This narrow bar of grassland between the wall of a decrepit building and the
eastern barren fields appears to be a rare outpost of greenery in this area. The
small well located here must have been dug deep with hard and tedious work in
order to reach fresh clean waters. A pelt, taut on sidelong poles, would provide
shade from sun and rain if not for signs of wear and small holes scattered
across the surface.
name: North of the Cot

desc:
What once was a yielding and fertile field of various crops has now turned into
a cracked and dry plain of sandy soil. The wind has fretted the regular furrows
into faint signs of a regardful hand that had probably grown tired of fighting
the relentless weather years ago. The planks that once formed a fence to keep
various beasts out have been trampled down and left to rot.
name: Fallow Fields

desc:
No trees or bushes grow here, and even grass has trouble taking root in this
hard soil, leaving these plains barren and easy to traverse. To the east the
ground rises steadily and forms a noticeble mound. In some places the earthen
blanket has slid away revealing a hard surface, on which stripes mark different
strata that have eventually formed this rise. To the southwest you spot a
coppice where, unlike in these barren fields, one might be able to find signs of
wildlife.
name: Plains along the Side of the Mound

desc:
Turning from the east to the south, the faint path meanders up a gentle slope.
There is no grass or plant life and no shade of green catches the eye, except
the distant forest far to the north and a few trees on the top of the mound to
the southeast.
name: Footpath Leading up the Mound

desc:
The surrounding scenery is somewhat bleak and barren, providing little solace
for a weary traveller. The path circles a number of trees that form a small wall
to the south, then continues west where it climbs toward the very same mound
that makes going south impossible, then follows the slope of the mound south
only to turn east closer to the top. The fields around the path are extremely
poor in plants and trees, but there is little doubt that more could be found in
the forest that can be seen to the north.
name: On the Meandering Footpath

desc:
Snakes and other creatures that find little solace in mighty trees and lush
grasses of more fruitful lands seem to be the few tenants of these fields. The
ground is regularly covered with small white stones that will probably soon
erode into featureless sand. In some places, however, someone has gathered the
stones, leaving noticeable empty spots. To the southwest a strange mound catches
the eye.
name: East of the Mound

desc:
The landscape here is considerably more barren than the outskirts of the forest
to the north. It is no wonder there are no animals to be seen for miles around.
The plants are stunted and the sparse grass is withered. The contrast between
this desolation and the viable northern forest might seem unfathomable, yet here
it is.
name: Withering Grass

desc:
Sparsely growing trees and bushes make travelling through such terrain easy, but
the fact that almost no grass grows in the sandy soil is probably a sign of
scarcity of water in this area. A cautious traveller would probably head north,
where nature seems to strengthen, and an adventurous one might travel south
where nature seems to provide little support for life.
name: Desolate Fields

desc:
Scanty slender plants and occasional patches of dried grass fail to remedy the
overall impression of desolation. These plains differ in no way from the vast
fields all around. Any traveller would have a hard time navigating here, if not
for the dark stripe on the northern horizon that marks the start of a forest.
name: Desolate Plains

desc:
Little plant life has managed to grow in the hard clay ground. The land to the
north is more fertile and a number of trees can be seen growing there. A long
and deep crevice lies to the east and on the farther side an old hut is faintly
visible. Regardless of the crevice's origin, it creates an impassable boundary
making it impossible to reach the hut from here.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
A fairly large hut, a lone structure in this border area between forest and
plains, stands to the west. From a distance the hut looks somewhat run-down.
Upon closer inspection it appears that not only is the hut frequently occupied
but also well maintained. The hut is built from a light timber stained for
protection from the elements and guarded by a sturdy wooden door. To the east a
well-trampled path forms a route through the trees and into the vast plains that
spread south of the forest of Lorien.
name: In Front of a Hut

desc:
Shaped by the passage of many travellers across the terrain, a path of hard dirt
packed tightly together leads through the plains. While the rains fail to dent
its surface, the soft ground to the side of the path shows the tracks of many
small critters that pass this way as they skirt around the path. A number of
tall trees grow just off the path, keeping it narrow and neat. Through the trees
to the north a low mound of earth is visible, while to the west the path leads
towards a hut.
name: A Tree-lined Path

desc:
Tall, majestic trees line both sides of this well-worn path as it winds its way
west and east across the fields. The way east is towards the Lorien plains where
the path is clearly visible, while to the west it winds through a dense patch of
trees and then vanishes.
name: A Tree-lined Path

desc:
A rough and narrow path runs east-west through the plains. The path is damp and
muddy, and while any footprints that were older than a day or two have washed
away, new ones have already formed. Many trees, tall and straight, line the
edges of the path. The trees continue to mark the path for many miles to the
west, but to the east they vanish a few miles from here as the trail dissipates
upon the open plains of Lorien.
name: A Tree-lined Path

desc:
A thin stream of water slowly winds its way southwards. The ground around the
stream is moist and fertile allowing a variety of grasses to grow long and thick
stems. A small path traces the brook and another appears to crisscross it as it
leads westwards through the trees. To the east the wide open plains of Lorien
dominate the landscape.
name: Following a Brook

desc:
Being very moist and fertile, the soil of these plains supports a lush carpet of
tall thick grasses. The grasses stand a good three feet tall and are dense
enough to impede the most agile of travellers. To the west a small brook can be
seen flowing softly across the plains.
name: High Grasses

desc:
The grass which covers this area is tall, broad and prickly. The sounds of small
animals can be heard as they move through the tall grass. To the south, east and
west the grass remains abundant, but to the north the ground becomes more rocky
and slopes downwards.
name: High Grasses

desc:
Several clumps of flowers, such as daisies, can be seen poking out among the
long blades of the thick grass. The soil is moist and fertile, as needed to
support the rich vegetation. The landscape is dotted by just enough bushes with
prickly thorns to hinder smooth travel. Far to the north a steep hill is
visible, while a single tall tree stands in the east.
name: Grasslands

desc:
This gentle slope slants from the summit of a small hill in the north to the
grassy valley in the south. The ground is moist and covered by dense patches of
tall grass. Where the hillside begins to ascend in earnest, some cracks are
visible in an otherwise solid rock plate. To the west the land dips into a
shallow bowl.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The butchering of the land here indicates that this was once an old quarry. The
rocky ground seems almost out of place as the surrounding areas consist of
rolling plains. The floor of the quarry was once white rock, but it turned a
dull green as it was covered by moss. Several muddy pools of water have
collected, but they do not look clean enough for drinking.
name: A Quarry

desc:
The endless plains are covered by a vast quantity of grass and small bushes. An
occasional tree, little more than a small shrub, can be seen between the bushes;
none of these trees manage to reach even four feet in height. In all directions
the terrain is the same with the exception of a few small mounds that rise and
fall without any visible pattern.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
A small brook runs from north to south, winding gently across the plains. Along
the brook a well-worn track has been shaped over many years of passage by
animals and people alike. To the west a large stone circle is visible on the
horizon; in all other directions the featureless plains continue.
name: Following a Brook

desc:
These desolate plains are interrupted by a number of large stones that are
arranged in a circle. The formation is too symmetrical to have formed naturally,
yet there are no indications of why or when the formation was constructed. While
an occasional sound, whether it be bird, land animal or wind, can be heard
across the plains, it appears that sound is unable to penetrate the circle.
name: Standing Stones

desc:
These fields are teeming with plants and trees. Grasses of various shades of
green form a rich carpet covering the soft, moist ground. To the east several
tall, oddly shaped stones rise in some kind of formation. In the north the vast
forest of Lorien covers the land.
name: Fertile Plains

desc:
The earth gently rises here, forming a low mound. While the mound might be the
remains of a burial mound, this could just as easily be the natural contour of
the earth. Regardless of how the mound was formed, several trees sprouted on and
around it many years ago, for now they are mature with a few of them showing
signs of decay. To the north the trees become more dense as the vast Lorien
forest begins in earnest.
name: Burial Mound

desc:
This is a fruitful area, teeming with trees and other plants. The trees, mostly
maples and other soft woods, grow more dense to the north, where the forest of
Lorien begins. To the southwest, some distance from here, a fairly large wooden
hut has been built.
name: Woody Plains

desc:
While the boundary of the great forest of Lothlorien lies to the north, a number
of hearty trees have managed to grow here, where the soil remains fertile.
Farther to the south, the density of the trees rapidly decreases as the plains
begin to form on significantly less fertile soil. To the east the woody plains
continue unchanged, but westwards the soil becomes sandy and the area barren.
name: Woody Plains

desc:
Only a few grassy tussocks can be seen in these sandy fields, where probably
only reptiles feel comfortable. A little to the north one can spot a green belt
of trees where the forest of Lothlorien must lie. Even the thought of such
powerful nature seems inappropriate, for here and for many miles to the south
not a single tree can be seen; however, the plains appear to grow more fertile
to the east.
name: Sandy Plains

desc:
Quite unexpectedly a slit opens in the ground here, revealing a small sparkling
underground rivulet that quietly seeps from between mossy rocks and vanishes as
it continues its secretive journey. Small bushes have gained some strength from
its soft murmuring, but the effects of the water do not extend very far. The
northern coppice is probably the source of the spring and most likely the water
is being absorbed by the southern dry and sandy fields.
name: At a Hidden Spring

desc:
A few mossy willows and birches rise toward the heavens here, marking the
beginning of the forest of Lothlorien. Some younger trees contribute to the
undergrowth amidst which small plants and occasional bushes spread their
branches and leaves. The ground is fairly dry, resembling the barren lands
surrounding this small coppice, except for the thickening forest north, where a
couple of muddy pools can be seen.
name: Outskirts of the Forest

desc:
Sparse bushes and dry grass form the outskirts of the small coppice to the east,
which in turn becomes a larger forest. The forest undoubtedly houses a larger
variety of plants and animals than these relatively barren fields. Large stones
encircle a small burnt patch where someone made a camp-fire, probably
considering this a safer place for rest than the lifeless fields to the south.
name: Fields along the Coppice

desc:
If not for the small white rocks gathered from the nearby fields, the footpath
would be barely discernible in the bleak plains, where only occasional patches
of wild grass grow. The hemmed path is the sole evidence of any activity. From
here it continues west and south, where the ground begins to rise.
name: On the Meandering Footpath

desc:
Compared to the barren lands to the south, nature on both sides of the faint
path seems much more lively and colourful. Even the ground here seems softer.
The footpath is lined with small white rocks gathered from the nearby fields.
Except for the stones and the chirping of birds of the northern forest, no other
sign of life or activity can be seen.
name: Footpath to the Forest

desc:
Occasional faraway howls come from the northern forest, mixing with the chirping
of birds and the other sounds of nature. The wall of pine and birch trees seems
impenetrable at first glance, but surely there must be a simpler way to enter
the forest of Lothlorien than hacking through this thicket. Looking south, one
can spot a small mound at some distance, and judging by some tracks leading in
that direction, the tenants of the forest may have found some easy way to travel
to the mound and back.
name: At the Border of the Forest

desc:
The northern forest echoes with the sounds of nature, yet there seems to be no
way to enter it. It would take days to hack through the thick undergrowth. Yet,
it might be wiser to camp in the shade of trees rather than here in the bleak
and barren plains, where beasts and reptiles of an unknown nature might be only
waiting for weary eyelids to close.
name: At the Border of the Forest

desc:
The ground is somewhat wet and muddy here, getting drier to the south but
getting even marshier as it begins to descend northwards. Drawing their strength
from the fertile soil, bushes and plants of different kinds seem to thrive on
both sides of the ditch. The merry chirping of birds and occasional howls can be
heard from the north.
name: Outskirts of the Marshy Forest

desc:
Some mossy willows and birches rise toward the sky here, marking the beginning
of the forest of Lothlorien. An unnatural and rather deep ditch opens up in the
north. Climbing down there could probably cause a couple of bruises. The ground
is fairly dry, resembling the barren lands south and east of the small coppice,
except for the thickening forest west, where a couple of muddy pools can be
seen.
name: Outskirts of the Forest

desc:
These fields end abruptly at a wall of trees and tangled bushes. Looking north
and south one could get the feeling of being in two places at the same time. The
southern fields seem to have no end in sight and no hill or grove hinders your
view of the horizon. Turning north, one can witness a seemingly impenetrable
forest that might perhaps be accessed through the western coppice.
name: Sandy Plains

desc:
The ground here is firm and dry. Though the forest is sparse, to the north it
thickens. The ground between the trees is littered with grass and several
saplings struggling in the shade. The vast plains of Lorien can be seen to the
south where they extend as far as the eye can see.
name: Outskirts of the Forest

desc:
This is the edge of the great forest of Lorien. The trees stand slightly apart,
allowing easy passage between them. In all directions, except south where the
plains dominate, the trees thicken as the massive forest takes shape.
name: Outskirts of the Forest

desc:
The trees that sparsely populate this section of the forest stand tall and
proud, towering high above. Noises from the forest wildlife can be heard
emanating from the rich undergrowth below and tree-tops above. While the forest
continues in all directions, it rapidly thins to the south and east where the
land has transformed into wide rolling plains.
name: The Edge of a Forest

desc:
Unlike the region to the north and west, this land is dry and infertile. While a
few trees have sprouted here over the years, those that have not withered in the
inhospitable soil are little larger than small shrubs despite many years of
growth. Between the few trees that continue to struggle, a tough hardy grass has
taken root. To the south the plains become more fruitful while to the north and
west a rich forest extends.
name: Plains at the Forest Edge

desc:
This area is the bottom of a large bowl, for in all directions the ground slopes
gently upwards. The rich soil allows the few shrubs that choose to grow here to
become lush and verdant. Far to the north is a very large dense forest, while in
all other directions nothing but plains can be seen from here to the horizon.
name: Dip in the Plains

desc:
A small brook runs north to south, making little noise as it flows over the
small pebbles and rocks that obstruct its path. The footprints of many small
creatures and of a few large ones can be seen along the path of the brook.
Looking across the brook, nothing but the spanning plains can be seen.
name: Following a Brook

desc:
Fresh, invigorating air flows across these plains as they extend in all
directions, slowly merging with the horizon. Long grasses grow all around
interspersed by a few small but hearty bushes. To the far east a small hill is
clearly visible against the skyline. While this hill is little more than a small
mound, on the otherwise flat plains it appears to be the only major landmark in
the neighbouring area.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The only feature which distinguishes this area from the surrounding regions is
the slope to the east that leads to the top of a mound. From the top of the
mound there is a wide, panoramic view of the undulating plains which extend
south of Lorien.
name: Sloping Plains

desc:
This hilltop allows clear vision in all directions, even though there is not
much except for the undulating fields to be seen for as far as one can see. The
ground around you is covered with small sharp stones that make passage difficult
for a barefoot traveller. Some larger boulders rise from the ground here and
there, half-hidden by the tall grass.
name: Top of a Small Hill

desc:
Here the ground is moist and fertile. To the south a small hill rises above the
surrounding land, however the slope directly south is far too steep to ascend to
the top. Perhaps the top can be reached from the west or east where the slopes
are less steep.
name: Near a Small Hill

desc:
A few small trees dot the landscape in this otherwise featureless terrain. For
many miles around in all directions nothing can be seen across a stretch of
barren plains. The sheer vastness of this area could make one feel small and
insignificant. To the north and west, at the tip of the horizon, the brown shade
of a forest can be seen.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
A thick carpet of grass spreads across the soil of these fertile plains. A
number of small shrubs and bushes dot the landscape, none of them tall enough to
be visible from a substantial distance where they appear to merge into the
grassy landscape. The air smells pure and unpolluted as does the water of a
spring that emerges from the firm ground. A small brook is formed as the spring
drains westwards.
name: Spring in the Plains

desc:
The ground here is dampened by the water of a small creek that runs from the
east to south. The creek's waters are crystal clear and flow almost silently
over the small pebbles in its path. The Lorien plains extend in all directions
from here except to the north where the beginnings of a mighty forest can be
seen.
name: Plains at the Edge of a Forest

desc:
Many different grasses and shrubs thrive upon these plains. A couple of trees
have also managed to sprout in the rich soil. The boundary of the forest of
Lorien can be seen to the north and west. To the south the ground dips forming a
shallow bowl.
name: Plains at the Edge of a Forest

desc:
Here the forest is fairly thin and the spaces between the trees allow clear
vision in all directions. To the north and west more woodlands are visible,
while to the south and east the rolling plains of Lorien extend seemingly
forever.
name: Forest Edge

desc:
The terrain around you is lightly wooded. Most of the trees in the immediate
area are elms, though a few maples and an occasional oak can also be found here.
The ground is littered with small saplings struggling in the shadow of the older
trees. While the forest continues in all directions, it thins out towards the
south.
name: A Light Forest

desc:
Many trees and smaller plants thrive in the rich, fertile forest soil that is
found here. Sounds of small insects can be heard, as they move about unaware of
the sheer size of the huge forest they occupy. The trees become less dense
towards the east and south.
name: A Dense Forest

desc:
The large number of trees growing in this area provide a rich food source and
shelter for many of the small woodland animals. Any travellers who happen to
venture this deep into the forest would find it difficult to make their way
through the thick roots that swell out of the ground. In all directions the vast
forest continues, obscuring your vision.
name: A Dense Forest

desc:
Thick moss covers all the rocks and infrequent fallen trees as well as willows
and birches stretching their branches up toward daily sunlight. The undergrowth
is quite thick and makes progressing in any direction tiresome. This is probably
the way it has been for countless years already, and if allowed it will stay
this way for countless years to come. A few fallen trees to the south make it
impossible to continue in that direction.
name: In the Forest

desc:
Recent land shifts or the work of some powerful minions have caused the ground
to be torn apart here, revealing a deep ditch. Like a wound that turned into a
scar, the soil is now covered with lush grass and bushes, some of them perhaps
yielding edible berries.
name: Lush Ditch in the Forest

desc:
To the north and west the forest gets thicker, while to the east the ground
slopes downward, marking the beginning of a deep ditch. Respectively, the
undergrowth seems to become more rank and the soil damper in that direction.
Animal noises coming from the east suggest that some of the dwellers of this
wooded realm might not be friendly at all.
name: Sloping Forest

desc:
The forest grows extremely dense, making it impossible to continue north or
south. One must be cautious to avoid getting tangled in thick undergrowth or
waving branches overhead. The obstruction north bears marks of an axe or some
other sharp blade, but in other ways it completely resembles piled trunks and
entwined branches placed there by elven-folk or nature itself - some might
wonder if there is a difference.
name: Through the Tangled Forest

desc:
A faint path leads you amidst tall trees, bushes and plants of different
varieties. This area is quite a contrast to the southern barren lands, as if the
forest was drawing upon some magical power of the land. Looking east and west,
one can see no other entrance to the forest. The path bears signs of both light
feet and hoofs, trodden equally by elves and animals.
name: Entering the Forest

desc:
Although one might decide from the scanty nature of this part of the forest that
this is its southern edge, a thick wall of entwined bushes obstructs your way to
the south. The ground is covered with small twigs and half-rotten leaves from
sparsely growing birches and with thorns and cones from pine trees. To the west
there seems to be a clearing, in all the other directions the forest continues.
name: At the Forest's Edge

desc:
Birches and golden mallorn-trees stretch their boughs towards the sky. The soft
wet ground is covered by an assortment of undergrowth. Lush ferns make it hard
for a traveller to mind his step, because one can see the ground only by pushing
the tall plants apart. You can spot the plants moving here and there, indicating
busy wildlife underneath.
name: Among the Ferns

desc:
The ground here is quite marshy, being fertile and containing excellent soil for
the ferns that keep most of the local wildlife under a secretive green carpet,
penetrated only by a few bushes. The white, black-dashed bark of tall birches
and the silver bark of the mellyrn is enlivened by their green and golden
crowns. A sound of flowing water comes from the north.
name: Among the Ferns

desc:
As the terrain varies, so does the vegetation. Here begins a sea of ferns that
extends westward where everything is covered by these thick green plants. The
southern forest mostly consists of conifers and smaller bushes, due to its
greater distance from the river, which one can hear flowing north of here. A
faint footpath seems to lead east, where an enormous mallorn-tree aspires
towards the sky.
name: Among the Ferns

desc:
Busy feet have trampled the lush grass into a soft lawn under this mighty tree
which could support the weight of numerous animals and even of houses that local
elves are rumoured to construct in the crowns of sturdy mellyrn. This very tree
may have witnessed generations of elves and their few but honoured guests smile
and grieve, laugh and mourn to the joys and sorrows of Lothlorien.
name: At a Mighty Tree

desc:
Much like rumours describe the elven tree-houses, this barn is built using what
nature provides, yet simpler and more robust, considering the needs of its
occupants. The soil is trampled by hoofs, and fresh hay and oats have been laid
where animals eat. Bridles, stirrups and other riding equipment on the walls
suggest that nearby dwellers are used to taking longer trips out of the forest.
name: Inside the Barn

desc:
Looking north and south, one can see the difference in the surrounding forest.
The more north, the less nature is afflicted by the intrusion of barrenness.
Whereas the trees to the south are of more common varieties seen in other parts
of Arda, the northern forest glows golden. To the west, the path is obstructed
by a wall of a building, while a similar but natural wall prevents one from
going east; yet no building exists in that direction.
name: Behind the Barn

desc:
This is a somewhat concealed lair, surrounded by natural piles of fallen trees
and entwined bushes. It must be the favourite breeding place for the wolves of
this forest. Branches and twigs have been carefully laid on the ground and
covered with leaves; these crude beds are surrounded by teeth-marked bones.
name: The Lair

desc:
Mighty trees aspire skyward, while smaller bushes and undergrowth balance the
sight of the beautiful forest. Several trees differ from the more ordinary ones
by their silvery grey trunks and golden crowns. The howling of wolves might
scare off the faint-hearted, but the forest seems to absorb some of the
threatening calls of these beasts. From the north, the soft murmur of flowing
water can be heard.
name: In the Dense Forest

desc:
Mighty trees aspire skyward, while smaller bushes and undergrowth balance the
sight of the beautiful forest. Looking north and south, one can see the
difference in the surroundings. While the southern trees are of a more common
kind that can be seen in other parts of Arda, the northern forest glows golden.
From the south, occasional howls of wolves can be heard.
name: In the Forest

desc:
The trees that populate this area are mostly mellyrn, though other types of
trees, most of them shorter, grow here as well. Far to the north the sounds of a
river can be heard. In all directions the dense forest extends, inhibiting the
vision of even those with the keenest eyesight.
name: A Mixed Forest

desc:
Here, deep in the forest, the trees are very tall. The long branches spread out
from the trees, forming a canopy over the forest floor. The ground, soft and
fertile, is covered by a carpet of green moss. Sounds of small animals and
insects can be heard both near and far.
name: A Mixed Forest

desc:
In this part of the forest the trees are of many different shapes and sizes.
They range from the small fruit-bearing varieties, some of which are little more
than a large bush, to the mighty elms which have towering trunks and spidery
branches. While the forest continues in all directions, it thins towards the
south.
name: A Mixed Forest

desc:
The trees that surround you are tall and majestic. A few trees have been set off
from the others by a series of gaps. These trees form a circle around a central
tree that towers high above all others. To the south and east the forest becomes
less dense. At a distance to the south the vast plains of Lorien are faintly
visible.
name: A Circle of Trees

desc:
Large gaps between the trees in this part of the forest allow for good vision.
Noises of many small forest animals can be heard from all directions. To the
north the forest becomes much more dense while to the south it thins until it
eventually fades into an open plain.
name: Sparse Forest

desc:
Here the forest is sparsely populated yet long ago, before a number of trees in
this area withered and died, it was quite thick. The shade from those trees that
did survive has made it difficult for new trees to sprout and grow. To the south
and east the land opens out into the vast plains of Lorien, while to the west
and north the forest thickens.
name: Sparse Forest

desc:
The forest to the north is enormous and encompasses the land for many miles to
the east and west as well as to the north. While the few trees that grow here
are relatively small both in height and girth compared to the mellyrn that can
be found deep inside the wooded area, they are still quite impressive compared
to the trees of the other forests. Looking towards the south the plains which
border this area continue until they join the horizon.
name: Plains at the Edge of a Forest

desc:
A few sickly blades of grass can be found growing between the stones that litter
the ground. A large pile of rocks has been placed under the largest of the trees
that grow here at the edge of the forest. To the north the woods become denser
as the forest begins in earnest, while to the south and east the fields seem to
roll forever. The trees to the west also become denser as they merge with the
large forest.
name: Plains at the Edge of a Forest

desc:
The ground is quite moist and covered by a dense layer of tall grass. A lonely
tall tree with thick bark towers overhead and is singular on these vast,
deserted plains. In the distance, far to the north, an immense forested area
spreads across the land.
name: At a Lonely Tree

desc:
This entire area is covered by a thicket of some tall grassy plants that thrive
in the moist soft soil. The ground is level and there are no landmarks except a
pile of old-looking mossy stones that sits beside a particularly thick clump of
grass. From the condition of the stones, it appears that they have probably been
undisturbed for many years. To the north lies the edge of a very large forested
area teeming with all kinds of plant life.
name: Plains near the Forest

desc:
A number of large trees surround you, many towering high over your head. A few
smaller trees, little more than saplings, can be seen as well, as they strive to
grow in the moist soil. The forest continues to the north and west, but to the
south the vast rolling plains start and continue until they merge with the
horizon.
name: Edge of the Forest

desc:
The trees here are tall and majestic although the forest here is less dense than
in the surrounding areas, allowing for a good view of the immediate
surroundings. The wooded area continues to the north, east and west, while to
the south lie the vast and empty plains of Lorien which extend as far as one can
see. While the plains are fertile only occasional mounds, which appear to be
little more than drifts in the otherwise flat soil, provide useful landmarks in
the level terrain.
name: A Light Forest

desc:
An ancient crumbled boulder stands here. Set in this place by the powers of the
world, it may have originated during the First Age when the first Elves were
awoken by the will of Eru. A slender young tree has recently penetrated the
stone and sprung forth with a natural beauty.
name: Crumbled Boulder in a Dense Forest

desc:
Under these tall fir trees, where sun seldom shines, a fluffy green moss
flourishes. The firs' trunks are moss-covered and grey from age and harsh
weather. Tiny trails, made by various animals, can be seen criss-crossing among
the trees. A small clearing can be found north of here, where the trees become
shorter and more sparse.
name: A Dense Forest

desc:
On this grassy slope fir trees grow among the tall grey-barked trees producing a
stunning contrast of bright and dull greens. On the ground a few small mounds
form the homes for the many tiny ants which are busy building their underground
tunnels. A small spring babbles nearby emitting refreshing water from the soft
earth. To the west a small bushy mound, which appears to be a mountain when
compared to the ants' mounds, rises from the ground. The forest continues
endlessly in all directions.
name: A Mixed Forest

desc:
A group of silvery-barked trees grows upon a small moss-covered mound. Their
tall trunks cast long shadows across the ground that is covered with grass,
bushes and tiny flowers. While to the north the mallorn forest continues
uninterrupted, to the east and south it thins out as the mellyrn mix with other
trees.
name: The Golden Forest

desc:
Fed by the waters of the river Celebrant, the mellyrn have grown tall here and
given seed to many saplings. Occasionally birds sing among the tree-brances.
Beyond the silvery boles a bend in the river can be seen where the rapid current
changes direction as it hurries away from its source in the east towards the
north. From here several tiny animal trails can be seen leading southwards and
westwards into the forest.
name: Woody Shore of the Celebrant

desc:
The forest extends in all directions except north, where the greenish water of
the river Celebrant flows eastward. Slender trees arch towards the river, their
dewy leaves showing a soft glimmer. While few strands of green grass have
managed to grow in the rich soil, many new saplings of the mallorn-trees
vigorously reach toward the sky.
name: Woody Shore of the Celebrant

desc:
The foliage of mellyrn is reflected in endless shades of gold in the pearly
waters north of here. Every drop of Celebrant rushes the journey east once, yet
united they can tell tales of joys and sorrows from the source of the first
mountain stream to the never-ending seas. To the west, the riverbank seems to
get marshier and to the south the forest deepens.
name: At the Shore of the Celebrant

desc:
Much like the steep shore on the other side of the waters that flow north of
here, this riverbank is covered with golden mellyrn. A small underground stream
seems to emerge here and contribute to the glimmering waters of the beautiful
Celebrant. Soft tussocks reveal tracks of some large and relatively heavy
animals that must have come here to quench their thirst. Judging by occasional
howls coming from the southern forest, the beasts might not be far.
name: Fresh Stream at the Riverbank

desc:
The muddy riverbank is covered with reeds. To keep from getting wet, one would
have to jump from tussock to tussock. Trying to ride here would be folly and
might result in a mount with broken bones. A natural ladder formed by tangled
roots can be spotted hanging above the river to the north. In all other
directions the golden forest echoes chirping of birds. To the south, fallen
trunks have piled into a natural obstruction.
name: Opening at the Riverbank

desc:
The riverbank is muddy and covered with reeds, which skilled hands might make
into light boats suitable for travelling on shallow rivers that require covering
some rapids by foot. Such troublesome waters can be seen to the north. In all
the other directions the golden forest echoes chirping of birds.
name: Opening at the Riverbank

desc:
Golden trees give way to what appears to be a beautiful meadow, where local folk
used to gather around fires to pass news and stories of great heroes and their
deeds. In less troublesome times the surrounding mallorn-trees would echo merry
songs and joyful laughter, though there is no doubt that some elves would prefer
to gaze silently into flickering fire and think of the great and safe western
realms of their kinsfolk.
name: Meadow of Stories

desc:
Formed by a mossy fallen tree, this pier extends into the River Celebrant. It
may be here that elven fishermen sometimes cast their lines, providing
nourishment for their families. The tree branches have dug deep into the
river-bottom, preventing the trunk from rolling over. To the north, below the
pier, one can see the waters flowing eastwards. In all the other directions the
golden forest continues. A faint trail leads south from the pier.
name: The Natural Pier on Celebrant

desc:
Wisps of fog escape from the soft turf when tussocks yield, disturbed by
travellers' passage. The River Celebrant that flows to the north dampens the
soil here, giving the flora an exceptional lushness. Tall mallorn-trees with
silvery trunks and golden foliage sparkle with unending brightness. To the west
you see a graceful tree arching above the river.
name: East of the Gracefully Arching Tree

desc:
This is a fairly small cave with a very low ceiling. The walls and the floor are
of a cold, hard stone on which there is a thin coating of white moss. There are
signs of animal life, such as the skeletal remains of many small creatures piled
up in the corners. The only way out appears to be upward.
name: Cave in the Hill

desc:
The tunnel comes to a sudden halt as it sharply narrows. Whereas the northern
wall contains a few specks of brownish dirt, the eastern and southern walls are
formed from a sickly grey substance. Although the cavern is quite humid, even
algae finds it difficult to grow on the clay surface. Only in a few spots where
once no vegetation could grow, now a few strands struggle.
name: Dead End

desc:
You are in the eastern portion of a long east-west tunnel. The walls of the
tunnel are largely composed of thick, stale grey clay with embedded pieces of
rotted wood. With the reduced flow of fresh air, some of the wood has petrified.
The stale air is motionless and ripe with the smell of decay. The floor is
littered with a few small bones and even a skeleton or two from the small
critters that once burrowed into the soil.
name: Underground Tunnel

desc:
This pit is little more then a shallow indentation in the tunnel. A pool of
dirty water has collected in the southern part of the pit. The walls of the pit
are composed of a thick, stale grey clay-like substance of a rubbery texture
that consists largely of ancient pieces of rotted wood. The air here is stale
but not as foul as to the north. The floor is composed of soft greyish clay with
a few brown specks. There is an intangible feeling that evil had once touched
this place and it may be many more years before this region is fully healed.
name: Shallow Pit

desc:
This is a section of a long and relatively straight east-west tunnel. The air is
stale and dry. Apparently the soil here turned foul and the trees above died,
