for dead roots hang from the ceiling. As the soil turned foul, the air here
filled with ammonia causing the small critters in the soil to die as well. To
the north these signs of decay recede, while to the east they grow more
frequent.
name: Underground Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel comes to a sudden end here. From the south to the north the space
narrows as the dirt walls transform from a sickly grey to a deep rich brown. A
few tender plant roots can be seen poking through the soft dirt at the northern
end of the cavern.
name: Dead End

desc:
This is a long and narrow tunnel with one end leading towards an opening above
and the other digging deeper underground. An old stench of decaying flesh comes
from the east where the tunnel slants downwards. The soil which forms a shell
around the tunnel is a sickly grey and would probably be poisonous to most
plants, though a few patches of algae, with the texture of mould, can be found
here.
name: Underground Tunnel

desc:
Once, long ago, the soil here was a rich and deep brown. Tall hearty trees
covered this land, as they still do to the north, and grew with great vigour as
their roots traversed the soil unhampered. Now that the soil has gone from deep
brown to a sickly grey, the roots lie rotting in the ill earth, with the trunks
they once nourished long dead and mostly decayed. The soil, now acidic, sags in
places, indicating the presence of an unstable base. In a few spots large holes
can be seen, and in other places it is doubtful that the weight of a small dog
could be supported for more than a few minutes.
name: Sunken Hole

desc:
The floor and walls of this hut are made of a plain wood stained dark. The hut
is composed of a single room which is mostly bare of furniture. The ceiling
seems solid and shows no signs of leakage. The stone fireplace against the back
wall appears to be frequently used for cooking food in the adjacent pot. A
supply of wood is stacked next to the fireplace.
name: Inside a Hut

desc:
Either by the recent spasms of Arda or by the work of some fiery weapon of the
minions of the Dark Lord, the ground has been torn apart here, revealing a deep
ditch riddled with dark pools of smelly water. The two earthen scars coming from
east and west meet here and continue south, where the ground seems to rise to
support the sparse forest. A pile of wood covered with rank grass and plants
bars passage to the north.
name: Swampy Ditch in the Forest

desc:
A platform has been built on the silvery-grey branches in the crown of this
mallorn-tree. The leaf-walls of the natural house are soft, yet unyielding,
fashioned in the true spirit of the elven-folk. Twined plants and clay-coloured
wooden carvings bear signs of devoted decorating, weaving their beauty and
lustre from a few magical whispers.
name: The Treehouse

desc:
Four walls and a roof seem to be all that is needed for the tenant of this
rustic building. Furs and trophy-heads of various animals decorate the walls,
and the furniture of the cot consists of a bed, a table and a shaky stool. The
bed is covered with fresh branches and leaves, and some food and drink have been
left on the table.
name: Hunter's Cot

desc:
The river Celebrant flows here, marking the border of the protected lands of the
Naith of Lorien. The river is deep but not too wide, and the water that flows
past is cold and clear. It seems that to drink this water would provide lasting
refreshment beyond the mere quenching of thirst.
name: Celebrant

desc:
The sparkling river bends from the west to the north. Fallen leaves float along
the surface of its crystal-clear water. Pale-green mossy stones line the bottom
of the river, almost reaching the surface of the water at shallow points near to
the banks.
name: Bend in the Celebrant

desc:
The River Celebrant bends here from the south to the east. The forest along the
western side of the river is thick and the trees overhang the river. Years of
erosion on the east side of the bend have moved the river somewhat, leaving a
silty buildup on the western bank.
name: Bend in the Celebrant

desc:
The Celebrant River continues its course here, making its way ever eastward. The
water is cold enough to chill any swimmer after only a few moments spent
immersed. The river is very deep here; even the tallest Elf would not be able to
stand straight up in the centre of the current.
name: Celebrant

desc:
Flowing in from the west, the river Celebrant makes its course toward the north
where it joins with a small tributary. The water moves fast as it approaches the
confluence. From here the sounds of the crashing water are clearly audible. The
river banks to both south and east have been undercut by the fast-flowing
waters, making it impossible to ascend.
name: Celebrant

desc:
Spilling down a low forested slope to the north, a tributary joins the mighty
Celebrant River that flows in from the south. At the confluence the joined
rivers become a single swirling mass of foamy water before rushing on eastward
with the combined force. The churning pool of water is not very safe to
traverse.
name: Celebrant

desc:
The flowing water of a tributary stream plunges down a low, but steep waterfall
here. The rushing current pours over slippery, mossy stones that make travel to
the north quite impossible. At the base of the waterfall, a wide pool has
formed, its glistening surface constantly rippled by the stream that plunges
into it from above, before streaming onward to mingle with the waters of
Celebrant to the south.
name: Waterfall in the Tributary

desc:
To the north this river appears to be little more than a stream, but here it
begins to widen as it gathers strength. It continues south, where it plunges
down a waterfall on its quest to join with the Celebrant that cuts through this
section of the Golden Wood, separating the sacred home of the Elves from the
outer forest.
name: Tributary to the Celebrant

desc:
The stream is moving at a greater speed here than it is farther upstream. It can
not be too far away from the point where it meets the main river. The clear
water that passes downstream seems to beckon you to take a drink; it is so clear
that stones can be seen, glittering on the bottom of the streambed and bright
flashes of fish scales frequently flash by at the periphery of your vision.
name: Flowing Stream

desc:
The river enters from the west and flows away to the east, cutting a straight
channel through the Golden Wood. The banks of the river are smooth and not too
steep. It is possible to enter the Golden Wood to the south, but the forest to
the north is thick and too difficult to traverse.
name: Celebrant

desc:
The River Celebrant continues on its straight course here, flowing from west to
east. Its north bank, crowned by great, thick-trunked, silvery mellyrn, is steep
and the land there is not accessible from the water. The trees to the south are
much smaller and thinner; they are beeches and elms, the companions to the
mellyrn of the Golden Wood.
name: Celebrant

desc:
On this stretch the river continues on its course from the west to the east. It
is a swift and cold river. Arising from the its chill source high in the Misty
Mountains to the west, it carries no dirt or mud to discolour its clear and
bright water. A steep riverbank looms to the north; it appears difficult to
climb.
name: The Celebrant River

desc:
The forest opens up to the river Celebrant both to the north and west. Across
the river, the Golden Wood seems to glow with a radiance that is less tangible
than simple light. The wet, mouldy leaves of elms and birches litter the ground.
name: Leafy Forest Bank

desc:
The forest is dominated by a row of large broad-leafed elm trees that line the
banks of the Celebrant. The river rushes by just north of this place, while the
large elms with their thick trunks overhang it, creating a canopy beneath which
the water flows. Intermingled with the darker trees are a few stark white birch
trees and a few mellyrn.
name: Row of Elms

desc:
The forest floor is wet and muddy here, most likely a result of river water
seeping up through the soil. The roots of the trees are exposed in some spots,
tangled in gigantic limb-like snarls that cannot find proper grounding in this
muddy area. A large elm has fallen, its root base sticking up into the air like
some grotesque spider.
name: Muddy Forest

desc:
The ground is soft and moist here, giving the plant-life in this area little
support for deep roots. Small, lithe birch trees have flourished here where the
heavier, thicker mellyrn and elm trees could not. The muddy forest continues to
the west. Drier ground can be spied to the east.
name: Clump of Birches

desc:
In this part of the forest a few great jagged rocks protrude from the earth. As
ancient as if left over from the time when this land was shaped by the great,
tumultuous battles of old, these spears of rock have been dulled over the ages
by both wind and rain. Still their looming presence sparks awe and wonder at the
powers of old.
name: Cluster of Rocks

desc:
The river rushes past to the north of here. The sound of rushing water is close
and loud, but the thick elms that line the riverbank make travelling to the
north quite impossible. All around, the dark-leafed trees spread their broad
branches. The forest continues to the west, south and east.
name: By the River

desc:
Swift and deep, the Silverlode, Celebrant in the elven tongue, flows by to the
north of here. The bank of the river is covered with mosses of various pale
yellows and greens. Perpetually slick and wet, the black stones at the edge of
the river offset the colours of the mosses. A cluster of birches can be seen to
the south. Large beech and elm trees loom to the east where the forest gets
thicker.
name: On the Bank of the Celebrant

desc:
The forest gives way to the river Celebrant, which flows past to the north and
west. The mossy bank is damp with moisture from the river. Dense clusters of
pale niphredil and yellow elanor sprout up on this rich and fertile river bank.
All around the mallorn trees grow tall, their foliage seeming like a reflection
of the carpet of golden blooms that blanket the ground at their feet.
name: On the Bank of the Celebrant

desc:
The woodland opens up by the bank of the Celebrant. The flowing waters stream by
to the south and east of this point of land that juts out into the river bend.
Light green mosses and ferns mixed with mallorn saplings dot the landscape, and
the steep descent into a narrow, but deep gully precludes travel to the west.
name: Forest by the River

desc:
The forest is of medium thickness here. The mallorn boughs stretch from one tree
to the next, blending into a living patchwork that extends all over this part of
the forest. The intertwined branches work to split the light from sun or moon
like a kaleidoscope, creating patches and slivers of light on the forest floor.
name: Golden Forest

desc:
This dense forest is populated with mallorn-trees, birches, occasional elms and
various low-lying bushes and shrubs. The foliage of the trees has clogged the
sky overhead, making this part of the forest a bit darker than the Golden Wood
to the north.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
The trees of this forest seem to glint and catch any rays of light from all
around, bouncing them off their silvery and pale white trunks and tangling them
in their golden crowns. The forest thickens to the north and east. To the south,
the trees thin out and the forest opens into a clearing.
name: Light Forest

desc:
The forest opens up into a clearing at this point. The large mallorn-trees have
stepped aside, opening the glade to the full radiance of the sun during the day
and giving many flowers and flowering shrubs a chance to flourish. Here the
yellow elanor, the pale niphredil and many other forest flowers fill the air
with their sweet aroma.
name: Opening in the Forest

desc:
The land slopes down toward the south. Barely audible among the noises of the
forest, the sound of running water can be heard from that direction. To the
north the forest thickens. Sweet woodland smells waft to your nose, not over-
powering but sweetly enticing, might be thought to beckon you deeper inside.
name: Woody Slope

desc:
The forest opens up here. The rushing Celebrant can be seen to the south and the
vast expanse of the Golden Wood lies to the north. The running water has slowly
eroded the water bank, leaving the deep brown silty earth exposed. Massive
tree-roots are protruding from the banks; if the river continues to undercut the
soil here, some trees may be lost to erosion.
name: Clearing at the Banks

desc:
The forest opens up dramatically here, exposing the rapidly flowing Celebrant as
it follows a course that began leagues away at its sources in Nanduhirion, known
by men as the Dimril Dale. The clear, cold water made a long journey from this
icy source above the dwarven halls of Moria, ere it came to this land.
name: At the River's Edge

desc:
All around the silvery mellyrn stand proud and tall. Occasional signs of
activity can be detected in the boughs above. Numerous shrubs and low-hanging
branches hinder passage beneath the trees. The thick, green grass that covers
the forest floor is deep and lush. It has grown quite tall, especially around
the thick trunks of the ancient mellyrn.
name: Grassy Forest

desc:
The forest opens up here to reveal a rocky outcropping blocking the way east.
Various ferns and ivy crowd around its base, and the outcropping itself is
covered with greenery. The forest trees have grown around it, and it seems to
have become a permanent part of the trunk of one of the trees.
name: Forest near a Boulder

desc:
The forest opens up by the bank of a small stream that runs just east of here.
Close by its banks little eddies play with clusters of mallorn blooms that float
on the surface of the water. Mixed in with these tiny blossoms are tiny posies
of elanor. A great mallorn bough leans out over the stream; a long rope tied to
it hangs lazily down, a thick knot tied at its end.
name: Golden Wood beside a Stream

desc:
This part of the forest is dominated by a veritable storm of little seedlings
that have been stirred up by the gentle warm breeze that blows through this
comfortable forest. They catch the light of sun, moon or stars and play with it,
dazzling the onlooker with a sea of glinting, floating shapes.
name: Golden Forest

desc:
The forest of Lorien spreads out all around. Crowning each silvery bole, the
green or golden foliage seems to shine and radiate warmth inside your very
being. A large cluster of elanor flowers grows here at the base of one of the
mellyrn, their faint aroma wafting through the air.
name: Golden Forest

desc:
The forest completely encloses the small glen, giving it the look of a sheltered
dwelling. Light barely penetrates the layer of boughs, and all sounds appear to
be muffled. Upon the base of one of the thicker mallorn-trees some marks have
been etched.
name: Forest Glen

desc:
The golden boughs of the mellyrn intertwine together to create a golden arbour.
The mellyrn are joined by the golden blooms of elanor and pale flowers of
niphredil that sprout along the base of the trees. Many other forest flowers in
pastel shades dot the grassy turf. Many small bushes and shrubs grow between the
trees, making passage through the forest difficult.
name: Bowery Forest

desc:
The trees part in a small sliver high above that lets in enough sunlight during
the day for the grass to grow thick and lush. This part of the forest seems to
be the beginning of a thick section that extends to the north and west. The
trees and shrubs that grow here impede passage to anyone not as light of foot as
the elves that dwell in the Golden Wood. To the south, the sounds of a running
river can be faintly heard.
name: Grassy Forest

desc:
In this part of the forest the golden mellyrn dominate, but smaller clumps of
birches and elms can be seen here and there. The forest floor is carpeted with
lush grass and the blooms of previous years. The forest thickens to the north
and west, but diminishes to the south and east.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
The forest trail continues on its path from north to south here. It never leaves
the side of the stream, as both travel from the northern edges of the Naith to
the southern edge of Lorien marked by the mighty Celebrant River which flows
down from the mountains under which the dread Moria lies.
name: Trail beside a Stream

desc:
A path comes from the north and seems to lose shape as it continues eastward. To
the east, the lush green grass of the forest trail gives way to a myriad of
grasses, leaves and twigs intermingled with some trees. There is a river to the
west, flowing gently to the south.
name: Fading Path

desc:
The Celebrant flows by just south of here. The banks of the river are steep and
firm. From the sides of the banks, the roots of the mellyrn protrude and dip
into the sweet waters of the Celebrant river that has dug a deep channel through
this forest. A barely visible trail runs from east to west among the trees. The
forest spreads out in all directions bar south, as far as the eye can see.
name: Near the Celebrant

desc:
The forest floor is uneven here, with great roots sticking out of it here and
there, impeding passage. Between the roots on the ground are what appear to be
many small stones lying among the graceful stems of the thick green grass. Upon
closer inspection it becomes apparent that these perfect ovals are the
silver-shaled nuts that have fallen in abundance from the mellyrn all around. In
all directions, the wide expanse of the Golden Wood extends.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The Golden Wood spreads out all around. Huge silvery trunks of the mellyrn
dominate the forest, their golden crowns reflecting and radiating all the light
around you to create a dazzling effect. It appears as if there is a path here,
leading west and south, but it is very faint and might be no more than an animal
trail.
name: Wandering in the Golden Wood

desc:
A great mallorn tree dominates this part of the forest, its branches overhanging
a hundred yards of the forest turf. The aromatic scent of its blooms is truly
pungent here, with blossoms from seasons past still littering the forest floor.
Upon closer inspection, a thin rope ladder can be seen, its colours merging with
the grey bark of the tree.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
Great, silvery mallorn trunks plunged into the deep, lush, green grasses of the
forest dominate the landscape here. The grass is so tall that it makes a loud
rustling sound whenever any creature moves through it.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
Amid the thick foliage and branches of the mallorn resides an elven home. The
flet is constructed of sturdy, smooth, polished wood. It has no railings; years
of living high above the forest floor have enabled the elven residents to move
through their vast network of flets without fear of falling down to the ground
below.
name: Upon a Flet

desc:
Hidden in the branches of a great mallorn tree, this flet seems to be the home
of a single elf. The surroundings are simple and even austere. A single table
with a chair, a chest and a small pallet are all neatly arranged on the wooden
platform. The pallet doesn't appear to be much slept upon, and the rest of the
furniture shows traces of only occasional use, suggesting that this elf might be
a hunter or a ranger.
name: Ranger's Flet

desc:
The forest is not too thick here but gets much thicker to the southeast. A small
mallorn grows here, dwarfed by its larger ancestors. Although the young tree has
a long way to grow in order to reach the heights of the tallest mellyrn of this
wood, the canopy of branches is not so dense here and it will receive the
sunlight it needs. Myriads of small bushes and plants flourish on the forest
floor. The niphredil flower has done exceptionally well here. It grows in
clusters, its white contrasting sharply with the deep green of the lush grass.
name: Light Golden Forest

desc:
The forest ground slopes down to the west. Layers of mallorn blooms and leaves
from the previous years make the trek in that direction a slippery one. A row of
mellyrn to the east and another to the west form a corridor from the south to
the north. Scents of many flowers fill the air.
name: Woody Slope

desc:
The foliage of the trees here is of medium thickness, just enough to give this
part of the forest just a little shade from the light of day. The silver-barked
mellyrn are joined by some slender and bright white birches. Together, the white
and silver boles stand like so many frosty pillars beneath the green and golden
foliage that comprises the leafy canopy above.
name: Dusky Forest

desc:
The sounds of the forest are readily apparent here. The rushing of water can be
heard not too far away. The chirping of birds and the occasional animal noises
can be heard from every direction. Many insects are swarming around.
name: Noisy Forest

desc:
The mellyrn that dominate this Elven wood are joined here by graceful elms and
tall, thin birches whose white papery bark peels at the touch. The forest is
quite dense, but many paths through the undergrowth make it easy to find the way
among the trees without getting lost.
name: In the Midst of the Trees

desc:
The forest is dominated by the sounds of the Celebrant that rushes past
seemingly not far from here. To the north, the wide graceful boughs of Elven
mallorn trees beckon, as if promising a quieter solitude than this noisy forest
near the river.
name: Forest by the River

desc:
The forest around you is filled with fresh air as a soft warble from the flow of
water carries on the wind. To the southeast the river Celebrant flows lazily
eastward, searching for a path that will allow it to join with the river Anduin.
The slender mellyrn, that densely pack this region, stand tall, with their long
branches concealing the sky.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
From this opening in the forest to the south one can see the swift Celebrant
unobstructed by the trees. It flows east, makes a turn to the north in the
distance and disappears to the northeast. Of all the mellyrn, a single tree
stands out. Its silvery grey trunk divides near the top into a crown, much like
others' of its kind. But the branches seem to have grown to the soft murmuring
of the waters below - they quite clearly depict the twists and turns of the
Celebrant!
name: By the River Celebrant

desc:
The forest floor drops into a narrow, but deep gully here. The east side of the
gully is too steep to ascend. Here the age-old mallorn blooms and other remnants
of past foliage have gathered, creating a soft pillow-like mat. The gully levels
out to the south, but it is possible to get onto higher ground to the north and
west.
name: A Small Gully

desc:
The tall mellyrn line the banks of the mighty elven river Celebrant. The cool,
clear waters of the river flow past just to the south and east of here. This
side of the river is the beginning of the Naith, and though the forest does not
appear to be any different from that on the far side, there is a calming sense
here that is not found anywhere else on Arda.
name: At the Banks of the Celebrant

desc:
The crystal clear waters rush eastward to a distant bend. The river widens a
bit, allowing to get a better view of the surroundings. To the north and south
the golden mallorn forest appears to continue endlessly. After the bend, the
Celebrant flows northeast, where forest streams make it even more powerful a
river.
name: Celebrant

desc:
This part of the underground cavern is considerably drier than the area to the
east. The floor here is comprised of soft, dry, silty soil. Bunches of dried
leaves are scattered on the ground, and large tentacle-like tree roots hang down
from the earth ceiling above.
name: Drier Cavern

desc:
The floor of this cavern is comprised of dark, cold mud that stains all fabric
it comes into contact with. The walls are literally seeping water as this
underground area lies directly alongside the river. To the north the cavern
continues, sloping downwards. There is a small alcove to the west whose floor is
elevated slightly.
name: Muddy Cavern

desc:
The water begins to get deep here, covering the floor in this part of the cavern
completely. The muddy bottom is thick and oozy, and anything that stands still
for too long begins to sink into the mud at an alarming rate. The cavern rises
up and partly out of this slick mess to the south, but continues its descent to
the north.
name: Watery Cavern

desc:
The cavern has become an underwater pool here. The thundering rush of the river
can be heard all around you, echoing off the walls. The river must be right over
your head now, and somehow this little cavern has remained unscathed. The
ceiling is dripping water like rain; looks like it won't be long before this
cavern is washed away entirely.
name: Underwater Pool

desc:
A round-topped mound crowned with mellyrn overlooks rolling forests in all
directions. More close at hand, a small trail wanders as well, but in this case
meandering from west to north. An expanse of soft, loamy turf can be seen to the
west.
name: A Tree-shrouded Knoll

desc:
The ground begins to rise and fall in gentle waves; tall trees cresting upon
hilltops with lesser stands below. Small rivulets of water trickle down from a
succession of finger-width springs, and the environs have a crisp, pleasant
demeanour all told. What appears to be a small animal path winds to the south,
then off to the east, deep into the fastness of inner Lorien.
name: Rolling Forestland

desc:
Nestled between two verdant swales, the ground dips into a shady vale where the
trees grow tall and stern, groping heavenward in search of extra sustenance. A
mat of large, golden leaves grace the fertile ground, no doubt blown here from
the golden forest to the south.
name: A Gentle Declivity

desc:
Lofty mellyrn stretch skyward, their elegant boughs interweaved to allow only a
trickling of light to pass downwards to the soft, moist turf of the forest
floor. To the south and west the Celebrant passes with gently controlled
fervour.
name: Under Golden Boughs

desc:
Flora and fauna burst with almost supernatural vitality, drawing sustenance from
the fertile soils deposited by Silverlode on her insistent path towards the sea.
The leaves of the trees seem almost to glow under the light of sun and moon,
refining and reflecting their illumination in some magical fashion.
name: Beside the Rushing River

desc:
Wide and gathering steam, the river sweeps all aside in its insistence to join
the great river, Anduin. Lorien hides both flanks of the river as it dwindles
into the distance with individual trees layered upon each other endlessly
blurring into varying shades of green.
name: The River Celebrant

desc:
The river bends to the east, and to the south and west, a mighty forest rises on
its banks. Some tree-roots hang over the shoreline and cast their thirsty arms
into the current. The water rushes noisily against the large rocks that lie in
the middle of the river.
name: Celebrant

desc:
A majestic, golden mallorn stands at the centre of a small clearing, hidden deep
inside a dense shrubbery. Tussocks and tufts of delicate grasses adorn the
ground, and white perennial flowers dot the clearing. Vegetation in the hues of
green, yellow, and gold conspires to form impenetrable walls around the
clearing; and indeed naught can be seen of what lies beyond. The golden boughs
of the great mallorn hang low and close to the ground, offering shelter and a
place for weary legs to rest.
name: Small Clearing at the Foot of a Golden Trunk

desc:
High in the air, a round flet rings the trunk of an exceptionally tall and
majestic mallorn. The flet blends seamlessly into the canopy, its construction
simple and elegant in its imitation of nature. A sleeping mat, several chests,
and a table occupy this abode, shaded by a dense roof of tangled branches.
name: Tethel's Flet

desc:
This part of the Golden Wood is filled with great mallorn trees and dense
shrub-like undergrowth. All about you the heavy leaf-laden branches droop low,
making it necessary for any man or elf to duck to pass below them. A faint path,
little more than an animal trail, winds among the trees, turning from the east
to the north where the forest opens up slightly. Hidden behind the trees to the
south, the River Celebrant sings softly as it cuts its course through the Golden
Wood.
name: Wandering in the Golden Wood

desc:
The forest is dense here, and the sounds of running water can be heard from the
west and south, but no water can be spied through the thick cover of foliage.
The golden mellyrn are thick here, interspersed with elms and birch trees.
Little light reaches the forest floor that is covered with the damp remains of
previous year's blooms and leaves.
name: Thickly Populated Forest
